title: Lycianthes ciliolata

The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions including wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Lycianthes ciliolata include alkaloids, glycosides, and other secondary metabolites, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made from Lycianthes ciliolata include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and other respiratory ailments.
